title: Why Your Online Safety Needs a Total Makeover in the Age of AI
summary: Have you ever played a video game where the bad guys keep finding new ways to sneak past your defenses? That's what's happening with cybersecurity! As AI gets smarter, it creates new ways for hackers to break into computer systems. An expert named Tarique Mustafa says our old ways of protecting data are like using a wooden shield against a laser gun. Mustafa is the CEO of a company that uses AI to fight cyberattacks. He spoke at a big tech conference about why we need to rethink security from the ground up. Instead of just adding AI on top of old systems, we should build security with AI at its core. Imagine building a castle: you wouldn't just put a fancy lock on a straw door. You'd design the whole castle to be strong. That's what Mustafa means. His company makes AI that can automatically stop data leaks before they happen. It's like having a super-smart guard that never sleeps. The challenge is huge because AI can be used by both the good guys and the bad guys. Hackers can use AI to find weak spots faster than ever. But if we build smarter defenses, we can stay one step ahead. The future of cybersecurity isn't just about stronger passwords—it's about teaching computers to think like detectives, always on the lookout for trouble.
why_it_matters: Just like you lock your bike to keep it safe, strong cybersecurity protects the apps and games you love from hackers who could steal personal information.

keywords.explanation: The practice of protecting computers, networks, and data from being broken into or stolen.
People who try to break into computer systems, often to steal information or cause damage.
Attempts by hackers to get into or harm computer systems or steal data.
When private information accidentally or illegally spills out of a computer system so others can see it.
Methods or tools used to protect against attacks.
People (or computers) who investigate and look for clues to solve problems or stop crimes.

background_read: Tarique Mustafa is the head of a company that uses artificial intelligence to stop cyberattacks before they steal data.
Hackers can use AI tools to scan millions of computers for weak spots in seconds, making traditional password protection outdated.
The conference where Mustafa spoke brought together tech experts to discuss how to redesign internet safety for the AI age.
Article_Structure: WHO: Tarique Mustafa, CEO of an AI cybersecurity company, speaking at a big tech conference.
WHAT: He explained that old security methods are like a wooden shield against laser guns, and we must build new systems with AI at the core.
WHERE: At a big tech conference (no specific location named).
WHY: Because AI allows hackers to find weaknesses faster, we need smarter, AI-driven defenses to protect data and stay safe.

perspectives.description: AI security can act like a super-smart guard that never sleeps, stopping attacks before they do damage.
Hackers can also use AI, making cyberattacks more dangerous and harder to stop.
The race between attackers and defenders is like a constant video game upgrade; both sides keep improving their tools, so security must evolve.
